assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13, the Test Quality row reads: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- 91% pass rate (54/56 assertions), 2 failing assertions in eval-3 (pre-existing: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002) |'. The verdict is WARN, not N/A or PASS, and the details explicitly state Eval Quality is WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13, the Test Quality row includes 'Eval Quality: WARN -- 91% pass rate (54/56 assertions), 2 failing assertions in eval-3'. The Eval Results Summary section (lines 57-58) states: 'The CI eval review (github-actions[bot]) reports a 91% overall pass rate across 5 evals. Two assertions fail in eval-3, both related to convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002.' This confirms the github-actions[bot] eval result was detected and processed with extracted metrics.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies reviewer-b's comment 50001 as a 'Suggestion' based on standard review comment classification rules. The file analyzes language ('Consider adding'), convention upgrade eligibility, and sub-task creation decisions -- all normal review processing. No mention of eval detection, eval metrics, pass rates, or assertion counts appears in the file. The report.md Review Feedback Summary (lines 53-54) lists comment 50001 as '| 50001 | reviewer-b | Suggestion | No sub-task created |', confirming it was processed as a normal review comment.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13, the Test Quality row reads: 'Eval Quality: WARN -- 91% pass rate (54/56 assertions), 2 failing assertions in eval-3 (pre-existing: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002)'. This includes the pass rate (91%, 54/56), the failing eval (eval-3), and the specific failing assertion topics (convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002).

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: The only sub-task file is subtask-1.md, which addresses the Documentation Coverage verdict mapping defect in SKILL.md Step 6a (a mapping to a non-existent 'Style Quality' report row). This sub-task was created from the acceptance criteria failure (AC7), not from eval-3's failing assertions. No sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures about convention upgrade eligibility or sub-task creation for review comment 30002. The report.md confirms 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created from review feedback' and the Eval Results Summary states the failures are 'unrelated to the Documentation Coverage changes in this PR' but no eval-specific sub-task was created.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md has a 'Review Context' section (lines 22-27) but it discusses the AC7 mapping defect, not eval-3's failing assertions. The Review Context states: 'The original review comment (reviewer-b, comment 50001) flagged the Markdown exclusion rule in Check 6 but did not identify this mapping defect.' It does not quote any eval-3 assertions about convention upgrade eligibility or sub-task creation, nor does it include evidence text about what was missing from the eval review.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 29 contains '## Target PR' followed by line 30: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. This exactly matches the required PR URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 6, the Root-Cause Investigation row reads: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created from review feedback |'. The verdict is N/A, not a non-N/A value. No eval failure sub-tasks were created, so no root-cause investigation was run on them.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md, and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. These are all verification artifacts (a report, review classification, sub-task description, and acceptance criteria evaluations). None of these files are code files. The subtask-1.md is a sub-task description file that describes what code changes need to be made, but does not itself modify any code. The report explicitly evaluates the existing PR diff without making changes.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md: Scope Containment (line 7) reads 'PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' -- comparing diff files against task specification. Sensitive Patterns (line 11) reads 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns in the diff; changes are entirely Markdown documentation' -- scanning diff for secrets. Acceptance Criteria (line 12) reads 'FAIL | 6 of 7 criteria met; AC7 fails due to incomplete Step 6a mapping' -- evaluating each criterion against diff content. The criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md files each evaluate their respective acceptance criterion against the PR diff content with no reference to eval results. These checks operate independently of the eval processing in the Test Quality row.
